plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-222 RP06.P06.M04.S24-RP06.P06.M06.S13 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-222 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP06.P06.M04 workflow tail (Claude bypass prompt, human approval note), the full M05 permission/audit binding cycle, and the M06 synthetic fixture head (40 rows across three sections) with Hermes evidence, Claude review packet, and CP00-223 handoff, without opening permission runtime evaluation, permission bypass, file download/share execution, AI runtime dispatch, DMS runtime dispatch, Hermes runtime receipts, review/approval route runtime, state writes, idempotency persistence, lock acquisition, workflow persistence, object storage, OCR, search, email, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-222 is a descriptor-only RP06 DMS Core "Permission And Audit Binding" slice (40 units, RP06.P06.M04.S24-RP06.P06.M06.S13). Verified via Read/Grep/Glob only. Coverage and deliverable/micro-title distributions reconcile across prompt baseline, registry.js requirements, validators.js, contract projection, and model.test.js; next-pack-queue.json confirms CP00-222 as the live current pack after CP00-221 (live_next_unit=RP06.P06.M04.S24). service.js builds frozen descriptor-only case sets with every write/runtime/real-data flag false; the ROW_EXTRAS chain (CP219->220->221) supplies security invariants (deny-over-allow enforced, no cross-wall/cross-tenant access, no leak, no permission bypass, no AI/file-download runtime, no Hermes runtime receipt) that validators.js, the validate script, and tests assert against the real plan. command-evidence.json and manifest.json correctly report production_ready:false / status implemented_pending_review with post-review gates deferred until claude-review-result.json exists - no overclaiming; the descriptor-verified flag is not an enterprise-trust claim. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false, human_final_approval_required. CP178+ hardening (forbidden_review_evidence, hardened_review_sequence) is defined and the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON (not auth_failure/0-byte/malformed). Hermes appears only as a deterministic review-packet/receipt descriptor, not product runtime (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). One P3 informational note on a benign changed-file-scope classification overlap; no P0/P1/P2 findings. Claude review remains read-only and is not final approval authority.

findings.title: Pack artifact paths dual-listed in changed-file-scope.json (informational)
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-222/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 116-119) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 121-129). The cp00-222/ directory is genuinely a pack deliverable (it holds manifest.json and command-evidence.json), so its appearance under a list named 'unrelated_dirty_files_preserved' reads as mildly contradictory. In practice the 'preserved' list is a superset of all untracked/dirty paths left intact by the scope generator (preserved = not cleaned up), and the generator separately and correctly tags the pack artifacts under untracked_implementation_files. validation-report.json confirms active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted: [], so nothing required was dropped. No impact on the production_ready posture, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, coverage, or authority boundary.
findings.recommendation: Optional only: consider renaming or scoping the 'unrelated_dirty_files_preserved' list (or excluding entries already classified as untracked_implementation_files) so the pack's own artifact directory is not labeled 'unrelated'. Non-blocking; safe to defer.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged as non-blocking and acceptable; no action required for CP00-222 closeout. The classification is functionally correct (artifacts are listed under untracked_implementation_files; nothing required was omitted) and the overlap is by-design superset behavior of the scope generator.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-222 dms core permission audit binding slice descriptor pack
